headline.purpose: Page titles; light weight (300) to keep tone soft.

body.purpose: Question prompts, option labels, result copy.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Hover state for cards, paired with translateY(-5px).

motion.name: option-transition
motion.value: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action across auth, survey navigation, and results.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2f5896; color: #ffffff; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #2f5896; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 400; transition: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background: #26456f; border-color: #26456f; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.65; }

components.name: Outline Option Button
components.description: Unselected survey answer option; fills solid blue when selected.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: #ffffff; color: #2f5896; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #2f5896; border-radius: 4px; transition: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover, .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { color: #2f5896; border-color: #091a33; border-width: 2px; }

components.name: Question Card
components.description: Fixed-footprint card holding a single survey question and its options.
components.html: Как часто вы чувствуете тревогу?
components.css: .ds-card { width: 320px; height: 480px; background: #ffffff; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 10px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; text-align: center; padding: 24px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-5px); box-shadow: 0 15px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.2); } .ds-card-title { font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.25rem; color: #2c3e50; }

components.name: User Header Bar
components.description: Logged-in status bar showing user name and logout action.
components.css: .ds-header-bar { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; width: 100%; background: #c3c9d1; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; margin-bottom: 16px; color: #2c3e50; } .ds-header-logout { background: #ffffff; color: #2f5896; border: 1px solid #2f5896; border-radius: 4px; padding: 4px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-header-logout:hover, .ds-header-logout:focus-visible { color: #227ccc; border-color: #1b2b38; border-width: 2px; }

narrative.overview: The app is a quiet, steady presence at someone's side while they check in on how they're doing — never a clinical intake form, never a gamified quiz. Density stays low: one question at a time, generous white space, nothing competing for attention. The palette leans on a single desaturated navy-blue (#2f5896) already established across every primary action in the product, paired with soft gray-blues and a warm-tinted neutral background instead of clinical white. This is deliberate: the existing blue reads competent and calm rather than cold, and the surrounding warmth (soft gradients, gentle card lift) keeps it from tipping into corporate-dashboard territory. This system explicitly rejects the unstyled-Bootstrap-starter look it grew out of — default btn-primary blue with no intention behind it, flat gray gradients with no warmth, and box-shadow values copy-pasted without a philosophy. Every token here exists because it's already load-bearing in the product; nothing is decorative.
keyCharacteristics: One steady accent color (#2f5896) carries all primary actions — no competing accents.
Warm-tinted neutral surfaces, not sterile white or cold gray.
Gentle, consistent lift on cards and buttons — tactile, not flat, not glassy.
Low density: one question, one card, one decision at a time.

rules.name: The One Blue Rule
rules.body: Companion Blue is the only saturated color in the system. It appears on primary actions and nothing else — no second accent color competing for attention, ever.

rules.name: The One Question Rule
rules.body: Typography never competes with the single question on screen — one prompt, one card, no secondary headline pulling focus away from it.

rules.name: The Always-Soft Rule
rules.body: Shadows are always soft and diffuse (large blur, low opacity). Never a hard, sharp drop-shadow — that reads clinical, not companionable.

dos: Do keep Companion Blue (#2f5896) as the only saturated accent color in the system.
Do use soft, diffuse shadows (rgba(0,0,0,0.1–0.2), large blur) for all elevation — never a hard, sharp shadow.
Do keep one question / one card on screen at a time; resist adding secondary content that competes with the current prompt.
Do reserve green (btn-success) exclusively for confirmed survey answers.
Do keep body copy warm and plain — short sentences, no clinical jargon, no diagnostic language.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color alongside Companion Blue; it dilutes the One Blue Rule.

Don't let the interface slip back into the unstyled-Bootstrap-starter look (default blue with no intention, flat gray-only backgrounds, copy-pasted shadow values) that this system explicitly moved away from.
Don't use a hard drop-shadow or flat, shadow-less cards — both break the "gently lifted, tactile" feel.

Don't use clinical/cold visual language (sterile white forms, hospital-form aesthetics) even on the heavier survey themes (anxiety, depression, burnout); the warmth of the system should hold across all themes.
Don't stack multiple questions or decisions on one screen; it breaks the One Question Rule.
